fields.label: Subdomain
fields.description: If your Zendesk URL is "grafana.zendesk.com", the subdomain would be "grafana".

fields.label: Authentication method
fields.description: Identifier of the selected authentication method for the Tickets service. The Zendesk API server exposes a single method, `basic_auth`; the backend defaults to it when unset.

fields.label: Email
fields.description: Email address used to login to Zendesk

fields.label: API Token
fields.description: API Token generated from Zendesk. Visit the [docs](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408889192858-Managing-access-to-the-Zendesk-API) to learn how

groups.title: Connection
groups.description: Provide information to connect to the data source

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials for the Tickets service (email + API token)

instructions.msg: Zendesk configuration is stored under a service-keyed jsonData shape: jsonData.services.zendesk.* holds per-service settings and jsonData.variables.<name> holds connection variables, while secrets use flat dotted secureJsonData keys of the form "zendesk.<secret>". There is a single service with id 'zendesk' (labeled 'Tickets').

Only one authentication method is available: basic auth (jsonData.services.zendesk.auth.id = 'basic_auth'). Provide the login email in jsonData.services.zendesk.auth.username and the Zendesk API token as the write-only secret at secureJsonData key "zendesk.password". The backend defaults auth.id to 'basic_auth' when unset.

Connection URL is derived from the 'subdomain' variable: the backend builds https://{subdomain}.zendesk.com/api/v2/ from jsonData.variables.subdomain. Provide the subdomain only (for the URL grafana.zendesk.com, subdomain is 'grafana'), not the full URL. The single server is fixed, so no server selector is shown; the backend defaults jsonData.services.zendesk.server.id to the sole server.

The secret at secureJsonData key "zendesk.password" is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether it is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields["zendesk.password"] == true rather than secureJsonData.
